title: Detect beads (br) and agent-mail availability at orchestrator startup
description: In orch_profile (or a new init phase in /orchestrate command), check if 'br' CLI is installed and .beads/ exists, and if agent-mail MCP server is reachable (curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/). Store availability flags in OrchestratorState (hasBeads, hasAgentMail) alongside existing hasSophia. When both are available, prefer beads+agent-mail over sophia for coordination. Add types to types.ts and detection logic.

title: Iterative polish loop with convergence detection
description:
Replace the single-pass refinement in orch_approve_beads with an iterative polish loop that tracks convergence and stops at steady-state.

## What to implement

### 1. Add polish tracking to OrchestratorState in src/types.ts
- polishRound: number (current round, 0-indexed)
- polishChanges: number[] (change count per round — beads added, removed, or modified)
- polishConverged: boolean (set when 0 changes for 2 consecutive rounds)

### 2. Rewrite refinement flow in src/tools/approve.ts
Current flow: user clicks '🔍 Refine' → one LLM pass → user re-triggers orch_approve_beads.
New flow:
- On entering orch_approve_beads, read state.polishRound (default 0)
- If phase is refining_beads, increment polishRound, compute change delta (see #4), display results
- UI options per round:
  - 'Polish again (round N)' — re-enter refinement
  - '▶️ Start implementing' — proceed to execution
  - '❌ Reject' — stop
- When polishChanges has two consecutive 0s, add label to 'Start implementing': '(steady-state reached ✅)'
- Cap at 12 rounds — after that, show 'Max rounds reached. Implement or reject.'
- Remove the inner while(polishing) loop — it's replaced by the state machine

### 3. Update beadRefinementPrompt() in src/prompts.ts
- Accept roundNumber and priorChanges parameters:
  beadRefinementPrompt(roundNumber?: number, priorChanges?: number[]): string
- Include in output: 'This is polish round {N}.'
- If priorChanges provided and non-empty, include: 'Prior rounds: Round 1: {n} changes, Round 2: {n} changes, ...'
- DO NOT add ultrathink or self-containment here — those are in the separate prompt-hardening bead (x5c)

### 4. Count changes between rounds
- Before returning the refinement prompt, snapshot open beads via readBeads(): store as Map<id, {title, descHash}> where descHash = simple length + first 50 chars (cheap, no crypto needed)
- On next entry to orch_approve_beads in refining_beads phase: snapshot again, diff against stored snapshot
- Count: beads added + beads removed + beads with changed descHash
- Store in state.polishChanges[roundNumber]
- Store snapshot in a module-level variable (not in OrchestratorState — it's transient)

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill's core principle is 'check your beads N times, implement once' with 6-9 rounds. Our single-pass refinement is the biggest gap. Convergence detection prevents both under-polishing and infinite loops.

## Key design decision
The polish loop is driven by the LLM re-calling orch_approve_beads after each pass (same as current flow), NOT by an internal while loop. The state machine (phase + polishRound) tracks where we are. This means:
- Each round gets a full LLM turn with fresh context
- The user sees progress between rounds
- Session restore works (polishRound is persisted)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] polishRound, polishChanges, polishConverged added to OrchestratorState
- [ ] beadRefinementPrompt accepts round/changes params and renders them
- [ ] Change count computed per round by diffing bead snapshots
- [ ] Steady-state detected (0 changes for 2 consecutive rounds)
- [ ] Max rounds cap (12)
- [ ] UI shows round info and change counts
- [ ] No overlap with prompt-hardening bead (x5c) — no ultrathink/self-containment here
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/types.ts, src/tools/approve.ts, src/prompts.ts

title: Integrate bv (beads-viewer) for graph health and parallel planning
description:
Add bv (beads-viewer) integration for graph health analysis and smarter next-bead selection. bv provides graph-theoretic analysis (PageRank, betweenness, k-core, critical path, slack) that our manual br dep cycles + br ready cannot replicate.

## What to implement

### 1. Add bv helpers to src/beads.ts
- detectBv(): boolean — check if bv CLI is available via `which bv`, cache result (same pattern as detectCass in memory.ts)
- bvInsights(pi, cwd): run `bv --robot-insights`, parse JSON, return typed BvInsights with:
  - Bottlenecks: { ID: string, Value: number }[]
  - Cycles: null | array
  - Orphans: string[]
  - Articulation: string[] (single points of failure)
  - Slack: { ID: string, Value: number }[]
- bvNext(pi, cwd): run `bv --robot-next`, parse JSON, return { id, title, score, reasons, unblocks } or null if no actionable items

### 2. Enhance validateBeads() in src/beads.ts
- When bv is available, call bvInsights() and use its Cycles/Orphans instead of manual detection
- Add warnings (non-blocking) for:
  - Bottleneck beads (betweenness > 5): 'bead X is a bottleneck — consider splitting'
  - Articulation points: 'bead X is a single point of failure in the dep graph'
- Return new field: warnings: string[]
- When bv unavailable, fall back to current manual cycle/orphan detection

### 3. Use bv --robot-next in src/tools/review.ts for next-bead selection
- In the 'user said looks good' branch (line ~240), when determining the next bead:
  - If bv available: call bvNext() — it considers unblock potential and graph centrality
  - If bv unavailable OR bvNext returns null: fall back to current readyBeads()[0]
- This only affects the SINGLE next bead case. The parallel case (ready.length > 1) stays unchanged — it already handles parallelism correctly.

### 4. Types
- Add BvInsights and BvNextPick interfaces to src/types.ts or inline in beads.ts

### 5. Tests
- Mock bv CLI output in src/beads.test.ts for: bvInsights with bottlenecks/cycles, bvNext with a pick, bvNext with no actionable items, bv unavailable fallback

## Key design decision: NOT using bv --robot-plan for parallel groups
After examining the actual bv --robot-plan output, its 'tracks' contain sequential items within each track (a track is a dependency chain). The orchestrator already handles parallelism correctly: it calls br ready to get all unblocked beads and launches them in parallel. bv --robot-plan would require extracting only the first item from each track, which is exactly what br ready already does. The real value of bv is in --robot-insights (validation) and --robot-next (smart ordering), not --robot-plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] detectBv() works with caching
- [ ] bvInsights() returns typed bottleneck/cycle/orphan/articulation data
- [ ] bvNext() returns the optimal next bead or null
- [ ] validateBeads() uses bv insights when available, with warning strings
- [ ] review.ts uses bvNext for single-next-bead selection
- [ ] Graceful fallback when bv unavailable — current behavior preserved
- [ ] Tests with mocked bv CLI output
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/beads.ts, src/beads.test.ts, src/tools/review.ts, src/types.ts

title: Extract commands into src/commands.ts
description:
Extract the 4 command handlers from index.ts into src/commands.ts.

## Commands to extract (index.ts lines ~311-496):
- /orchestrate (line ~311)
- /orchestrate-stop (line ~344) 
- /orchestrate-status (line ~364)
- /memory (line ~376)

## Pattern
Each command becomes an exported function:
```typescript
import type { OrchestratorContext } from './types.js';
export function registerCommands(oc: OrchestratorContext) {
  oc.pi.registerCommand('orchestrate', { ... });
  // etc
}
```

The function takes OrchestratorContext and registers all commands.
index.ts calls registerCommands(oc) after creating the context.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] src/commands.ts exists with all 4 command handlers
- [ ] index.ts imports and calls registerCommands()
- [ ] Command handlers access state/pi/etc via OrchestratorContext
- [ ] npm run build passes
- [ ] npm test passes

## What to implement

title: Add empty/shallow description validation to validateBeads
description:
Extend validateBeads() in src/beads.ts to check for beads with empty or very shallow descriptions.

## What to implement
- After cycle and orphan checks, iterate open beads
- Flag beads where description is empty or under 50 characters
- Flag beads missing '### Files:' section
- Return new field in validation result: shallowBeads: { id: string, reason: string }[]
- Update callers (approve.ts) to display shallow bead warnings

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill validates: br list --json | jq '.[] | select(.description == "")'. We check for cycles and orphans but never for empty/shallow descriptions. A bead without substance will produce garbage implementation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Empty descriptions flagged
- [ ] Descriptions under 50 chars flagged

title: Replace compound memory with CASS memory system
description:
Replace the current simple file-based compound memory (src/memory.ts) with CASS memory system (cm CLI).

## What to implement
- Rewrite src/memory.ts to wrap cm CLI commands instead of raw file I/O
- readMemory() → cm context <task> --json (returns relevant bullets, anti-patterns, history)
- appendMemory() → cm append or cm reflect to store learnings
- listMemoryEntries() → cm list --json
- searchMemory() → cm similar <query> --json
- pruneMemoryEntries() → cm mark --harmful or cm prune
- getMemoryStats() → cm doctor --json
- Add detectCass() helper: check if cm CLI is available, return boolean
- Graceful fallback: if cm not installed, return empty/no-op (same as current missing-file behavior)
- Keep the same exported API surface so callers don't break

## Why it matters
CASS provides relevance-scored contextual memory, anti-pattern tracking, cross-agent memory sharing, and trauma guard — far richer than flat markdown append.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] src/memory.ts wraps cm CLI for all operations
- [ ] detectCass() returns true when cm is available, false otherwise
- [ ] All functions gracefully degrade when cm unavailable (return empty results, not throw)
- [ ] Existing tests in src/memory.test.ts updated to mock cm CLI calls
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

title: Epic: Replace PlanStep with beads+agent-mail as core execution substrate
description:
Fundamental architectural shift: beads (br CLI) become THE planning and execution primitive. Agent-mail handles all coordination. PlanStep type and orch_plan tool are removed entirely.

## Why
- The orchestrator currently has its own PlanStep abstraction that duplicates what beads already do (tasks, deps, status lifecycle)
- Agent-mail provides file reservations and messaging that the orchestrator reimplements with worktree hacks
- The Idea Wizard skill creates beads natively — forcing ideas through PlanStep is a lossy translation
- Beads are git-visible, crash-recoverable, and queryable via br CLI

## Architecture after migration
- Discovery (all modes) → idea generation → br create beads with deps
- Planning IS bead creation — no separate orch_plan tool
- Execution reads br ready --json for actionable beads
- Parallel groups derived from bead dep graph
- orch_review works with bead IDs, br close on pass
- Agent-mail threads scoped to bead IDs for review trails
- br sync --flush-only at every state transition

## What gets removed
- PlanStep type, Plan type
- orch_plan tool entirely  
- analyzeParallelGroups() (replaced by br dep graph)
- createBeadsFromPlan() (beads are created directly, not derived from PlanSteps)

title: Add Bead types and new state fields to types.ts (additive, no removals)
description:
Phase 1: ADD new bead-centric types alongside existing ones (don't remove PlanStep yet — that breaks everything).

Add to types.ts:
- Bead interface matching br list --json output: { id, title, description, status, priority, type, labels, estimate }
- BeadResult interface: { beadId: string, status: 'success' | 'partial' | 'blocked', summary: string }
- BeadReview interface: { beadId: string, passed: boolean, feedback: string, revisionInstructions?: string }

Add NEW fields to OrchestratorState (keep old ones for now):
- activeBeadIds?: string[] — bead IDs created for this orchestration
- beadResults?: Record<string, BeadResult>
- beadReviews?: Record<string, BeadReview>
- currentBeadId?: string | null

Add new phases to OrchestratorPhase:
- 'creating_beads' — LLM is calling br create
- 'refining_beads' — Phase 6 interactive refinement loop
- 'awaiting_bead_approval' — user reviews bead list before execution

DO NOT remove PlanStep, Plan, or any existing state fields yet. That happens in the final cleanup bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bead, BeadResult, BeadReview interfaces defined
- New state fields added to OrchestratorState
- New phases added to OrchestratorPhase
- createInitialState() updated with new field defaults
- Existing PlanStep/Plan types still present (no breakage)
- npm run build passes

title: Remove orch_plan tool, add bead approval + Phase 6 refinement + execution launch
description:
This replaces the entire orch_plan registerTool block. The new flow:

1. BEAD APPROVAL: After the LLM creates beads (phase creating_beads), the orchestrator reads br list --json and presents them to the user. UI options:
   - 'Start implementing' → proceed to execution
   - 'Refine beads' → Phase 6 refinement (interactive)
   - 'Reject' → stop

2. PHASE 6 REFINEMENT (interactive): Emit beadRefinementPrompt() telling the LLM to review beads via br list, refine with br update, check with bv. After each pass, re-present the approval UI. User triggers each pass manually (not automated).

3. EXECUTION LAUNCH: On approval, read br ready --json for first batch of executable beads. Determine parallel groups from bead dep graph. Launch execution via parallel_subagents (keyed by bead ID, not step index).

Implementation approach: This is a NEW tool or event handler — not a modification of orch_plan (which gets deleted). Could be:
- A new orch_approve_beads tool the LLM calls after creating beads
- Or automatic: the orchestrator detects phase=creating_beads completion and triggers approval UI

The automatic approach is better — fewer tool calls, less chance of the LLM forgetting.

How to detect completion: After the LLM's response ends while phase=creating_beads, inject a before_agent_start hook or use pi.on('after_turn') to read br list --json and transition to awaiting_bead_approval.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_plan registerTool block completely removed
- Bead approval UI shows br list output with deps
- Phase 6 refinement is interactive (user-triggered passes)
- On approval, br ready --json determines first executable batch
- Execution launch works with bead IDs (parallel_subagents keyed by bead ID)
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite beads.ts: remove createBeadsFromPlan, add readBeads/readyBeads/beadToTask helpers
description:
beads.ts currently has createBeadsFromPlan() which translates PlanSteps → beads. This is backwards — beads should be created directly by the LLM via br create.

Replace with:
- readBeads(pi, cwd): parse br list --json, return Bead[]
- readyBeads(pi, cwd): parse br ready --json, return Bead[] (open, unblocked, not deferred)
- getBeadById(pi, cwd, id): br get <id> --json → Bead
- beadDeps(pi, cwd, id): br dep list <id> --json → string[] (parent bead IDs)
- updateBeadStatus(pi, cwd, id, status) — already exists, keep
- syncBeads(pi, cwd) — already exists, keep
- validateBeads(pi, cwd) — already exists, keep  
- getBeadsSummary — update to work from Bead[], not stepIndex map

Remove:
- createBeadsFromPlan() entirely

Acceptance criteria:
- No reference to PlanStep in beads.ts
- readBeads and readyBeads return Bead[] from br CLI
- All helpers use pi.exec('br', ...) with --json flag
- validateBeads still checks for cycles via br dep cycles

title: Rewrite prompts.ts: remove plannerInstructions, add bead-creation prompts from Idea Wizard phases
description:
prompts.ts has ~15 functions, many using PlanStep. Full inventory of changes:

REMOVE:
- plannerInstructions() — replaced by beadCreationPrompt
- planToTasksInstructions() — beads ARE tasks, no conversion needed
- parallelExecutionInstructions() — parallel groups now come from br ready

REWRITE to use Bead instead of PlanStep:
- implementerInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → implementerInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- reviewerInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → reviewerInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- adversarialReviewInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → adversarialReviewInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- crossAgentReviewInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- realityCheckInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- commitStrategyInstructions(steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- summaryInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- skillExtractionInstructions() — no PlanStep dependency, keep as-is
- polishInstructions() — no PlanStep dependency, keep as-is

ADD:
- beadCreationPrompt(goal, repoContext, constraints): Phase 5 exact wording from Idea Wizard skill. Tells LLM to use br create / br dep add directly. Must include:
  * br create syntax with --description, --parent, -t, -p flags
  * br dep add syntax
  * Instruction to make beads self-documenting (background, reasoning, acceptance criteria)
  * Scope: each bead should specify which files it touches (in description or labels)
- beadRefinementPrompt(): Phase 6 exact wording. Tells LLM to review via br list, refine with br update, validate with br dep cycles. Includes 'DO NOT OVERSIMPLIFY' constraint.

KEEP UNCHANGED:
- formatRepoProfile(), discoveryInstructions(), orchestratorSystemPrompt(), goalRefinementPrompt()

UPDATE:
- orchestratorSystemPrompt(): change workflow step 4 from 'call orch_plan' to 'create beads via br create'. Remove all orch_plan references. Update system prompt to describe bead-centric workflow.

Acceptance criteria:
- plannerInstructions, planToTasksInstructions, parallelExecutionInstructions removed
- All remaining prompt functions use Bead/BeadResult instead of PlanStep/StepResult
- beadCreationPrompt includes Phase 5 exact wording
- beadRefinementPrompt includes Phase 6 exact wording
- orchestratorSystemPrompt describes bead workflow, not orch_plan workflow
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite orch_select and custom-goal path to emit bead-creation prompts
description:
After orch_select picks a goal (or custom goal is entered), the flow currently calls plannerInstructions() and tells the LLM to call orch_plan. Replace with:

1. orch_select: After goal selection, emit beadCreationPrompt() telling the LLM to create beads via 'br create' / 'br dep add' in bash. Set phase to 'creating_beads'.

2. Custom goal path (in orch_profile, the pencil-emoji branch): Same change — emit beadCreationPrompt() instead of plannerInstructions().

3. Deep plan mode: Instead of spawning 3 LLMs that produce PlanStep arrays, spawn 3 LLMs that each create beads via br create. The synthesis step merges/deduplicates the best beads.

4. Creative brainstorm mode: Brainstorm agents produce ideas as text (no change). The user picks ideas, then the LLM creates beads for the selected ideas.

The key pattern: the LLM calls br create/br dep add directly in bash. No orch_plan tool. The orchestrator detects bead creation by reading br list --json after the LLM finishes.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_select returns beadCreationPrompt instead of plannerInstructions
- Custom goal path returns beadCreationPrompt instead of plannerInstructions
- Deep plan mode spawns agents that create beads (not PlanStep arrays)
- Phase transitions to 'creating_beads' after goal selection
- No references to orch_plan in orch_select or orch_profile custom-goal path
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite orch_review to work with bead IDs instead of step indices
description:
orch_review currently takes stepIndex: number and works against state.plan.steps. Rewrite to:

PARAMETER CHANGES:
- beadId: string instead of stepIndex: number (primary identifier)
- summary, verdict, feedback, revisionInstructions — same as before

ON PASS:
- updateBeadStatus(beadId, 'closed') via beads.ts
- syncBeads()
- Merge worktree if applicable (use bead ID to find worktree, not step index)
- Agent-mail: send review result as message in bead's thread (thread_id = beadId)

ON FAIL:
- Keep bead open (don't update status)
- Record in state.beadReviews[beadId]
- Increment retry count

PARALLEL GROUP ADVANCEMENT:
After a bead passes, call readyBeads() to check if new beads became unblocked. If yes, launch them.
This replaces the analyzeParallelGroups group-tracking logic entirely — br ready IS the group computation.

GUIDED GATES (runGuidedGates):
Currently references state.plan!.steps and allArtifacts from PlanStep. Rewrite to:
- Get all artifacts from bead descriptions (parse file paths) or from beadIds list
- Replace step-index references with bead IDs in agent task prompts
- Sentinel value for entering guided gates: use a special beadId like '__gates__' instead of stepIndex = steps.length + 1

HIT-ME AGENTS:
- hitMeTriggered and hitMeCompleted keyed by bead ID, not step index
- Agent task prompts reference bead description, not step description
- Agent-mail thread_id = beadId for review threads

IMPORTANT: The 'looks good → advance' logic (lines ~2140-2300) is the most complex part.
Current flow: find unreviewed steps → check parallel groups → launch next group → or advance sequentially.
New flow: readyBeads() → if any ready → launch them (parallel if multiple, sequential if one). Much simpler.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_review parameter schema uses beadId: string not stepIndex: number
- Pass → updateBeadStatus closed, Fail → stays open  
- syncBeads called after every review
- Advancement uses readyBeads() instead of analyzeParallelGroups
- Guided gates work with bead IDs
- Hit-me agents reference bead IDs
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite parallel execution to use br ready + agent-mail, remove analyzeParallelGroups
description:
Replace analyzeParallelGroups (in sophia.ts) with br ready-based execution.

REMOVE from sophia.ts:
- analyzeParallelGroups() function  
- resolveDependencies() helper (only used by analyzeParallelGroups)
- ParallelAnalysis interface
- All PlanStep imports in sophia.ts

REMOVE from agent-mail.ts:
- groupArtifactsAreDisjoint(group, steps: PlanStep[]) — rewrite to work with Bead[]

REMOVE from index.ts:
- parallelAnalysis variable and all references
- sameDirGroups tracking
- isSameDirStep() helper

NEW EXECUTION LOOP (in index.ts or new execution.ts):
The execution loop becomes radically simpler:

  while true:
    ready = readyBeads(pi, cwd)
    if ready.length === 0: break  // all done or all blocked
    
    if ready.length === 1:
      // Sequential: implement single bead
      emit implementerInstructions(ready[0])
    else:
      // Parallel: check artifact overlap
      if artifactsDisjoint(ready) && agentMailAvailable:
        launch same-dir with file reservations
      else:
        launch with worktrees
    
    wait for completion
    orch_review each bead

ARTIFACT EXTRACTION from beads:
Beads don't have a structured 'artifacts' field like PlanStep did. Options:
1. Parse file paths from bead description (fragile)
2. Use bead labels as artifact tags (e.g. labels: ['file:src/types.ts', 'file:src/beads.ts'])
3. Require beadCreationPrompt to instruct LLM to put artifacts in a structured section of the description

Option 3 is best: the bead creation prompt tells the LLM to include '### Files: src/foo.ts, src/bar.ts' in every bead description. The orchestrator parses this section.

Acceptance criteria:
- analyzeParallelGroups, resolveDependencies, ParallelAnalysis removed from sophia.ts
- groupArtifactsAreDisjoint rewritten for Bead[] in agent-mail.ts
- Execution uses readyBeads() loop
- Artifact extraction from bead descriptions works
- WorktreePool still works as fallback for overlapping artifacts
- npm run build passes

title: Update session restore and /orchestrate-status to work with beads
description:
Session restore currently rebuilds state from PlanStep data. Update to:

Session restore:
- Read bead state from br list --json (beads survive crashes — they're on disk)
- Reconstruct beadResults/beadReviews from bead statuses
- Determine current phase from bead states: all open = creating_beads, some in_progress = implementing, all closed = complete
- No need to persist plan in session state — beads ARE the persisted plan

/orchestrate-status:
- Show bead summary from br list --json (open/in_progress/closed counts)
- Show current phase
- Show ready beads (next to execute)
- Show coordination backend info

This is actually simpler than current restore because beads are the source of truth, not session JSON.

Acceptance criteria:
- Session restore reads from br list --json
- Phase reconstruction works from bead statuses
- /orchestrate-status shows bead-based progress
- No references to state.plan in restore or status code
- npm run build passes

title: Update all tests: beads.test.ts, coordination.test.ts, scan.test.ts, add integration tests
description:
Update test suite for bead-centric architecture:

beads.test.ts:
- Remove createBeadsFromPlan tests
- Add readBeads/readyBeads tests (mock br list --json / br ready --json)
- Add getBeadById tests

New: integration.test.ts (or update existing):
- Test full flow: create beads → read ready → execute → review → close → next batch
- Mock br CLI calls throughout
- Test Phase 6 refinement loop
- Test agent-mail thread creation per bead

Remove:
- Any test referencing PlanStep or orch_plan

Acceptance criteria:
- npm test passes
- No PlanStep references in any test file  
- beads.test.ts covers readBeads, readyBeads, getBeadById

title: Quality checklist gate with description validation
description:
Add an automated quality gate that runs before implementation starts, combining shallow-description detection with the full beads-workflow quality checklist.

## What to implement

### 1. Add qualityCheckBeads() to src/beads.ts
Validates each open bead against these checks:
1. **Has substance**: description.length >= 100 chars (heuristic — short descriptions produce poor implementations)
2. **Has file scope**: description contains '### Files:' with at least one path
3. **Has acceptance criteria**: description contains '- [ ]' checkbox markers
4. **Not oversimplified**: description word count >= 50
5. **No cycles**: reuse existing validateBeads() cycle check
6. **Dependencies connected**: bead either has deps, is depended on, or is the sole bead (skip for single-bead plans)

Return: { passed: boolean, failures: { beadId: string, check: string, reason: string }[] }

Note: 'Preserves features' and 'Includes tests' are inherently subjective — they stay as polish-prompt questions, not automated checks.

### 2. Update validateBeads() return type in src/beads.ts
- Add shallowBeads: { id: string, reason: string }[] to the return value
- Populate during validation: flag empty descriptions, < 50 chars, missing ### Files: section

### 3. Wire quality gate into approve.ts
- When user clicks 'Start implementing', call qualityCheckBeads() first
- If failures exist, display them formatted:
  '⚠️ Quality issues found:\n- bead-123: No acceptance criteria (missing - [ ] checkboxes)\n- bead-456: Description too short (23 chars)'
- Offer choices: 'Go back to polish', 'Proceed anyway'
- If all pass, proceed normally (no extra prompt)

### 4. Show quality summary in bead list during approval
- Add a line like '✅ 5/5 beads pass quality checks' or '⚠️ 3/5 pass — 2 issues found' to the approval display

### 5. Tests
- Unit test qualityCheckBeads() with mock beads: one valid, one empty description, one missing files, one missing acceptance criteria
- Test that validateBeads returns shallowBeads

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill has an explicit 8-item quality checklist. Without automated validation, shallow beads slip through and produce garbage implementations requiring expensive rework.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] qualityCheckBeads() validates all 6 automated checks
- [ ] validateBeads() returns shallowBeads alongside cycles/orphans
- [ ] Gate runs on 'Start implementing' in approve.ts
- [ ] Failures displayed with specific bead IDs, check names, and reasons
- [ ] User can override ('Proceed anyway')
- [ ] Quality summary shown in bead list display
- [ ] Tests cover valid beads, empty descriptions, missing files, missing criteria
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/beads.ts, src/beads.test.ts, src/tools/approve.ts

Index.ts registerTool calls delegate to imports.

title: Sync beads after each polish round
description:
Call syncBeads() (br sync --flush-only) after each polish refinement pass to persist bead state to .beads/ JSONL files.

## What to implement
- In src/tools/approve.ts: after the LLM returns from a refinement pass and before re-entering the approval UI, call syncBeads(oc.pi, ctx.cwd)
- This ensures polish changes survive process death or session interruption

## Why it matters
Currently syncBeads() is only called at approval time. If the process dies during polish round 5, all 5 rounds of refinement are lost. The beads-workflow skill explicitly calls br sync --flush-only + git add .beads/ after changes. We should at minimum sync after each round.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] syncBeads called after each refinement pass returns
- [ ] Bead state persisted to .beads/ after every polish round
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

Pass state + dependencies as explicit parameters rather than closing over module-level variables.

title: Support subtask hierarchy in bead creation
description:
Enhance bead creation and display to support parent-child subtask relationships.

## Current state
- The Bead type in src/types.ts ALREADY has a `parent?: string` field
- br CLI supports `br dep add child parent --type parent-child` for subtask relationships
- The orchestrator currently ignores parent-child structure — all beads are displayed and executed as a flat list

## What to implement

### 1. Update beadCreationPrompt() in src/prompts.ts
Add to the Instructions section:
```
For complex beads that would take more than a few hours, break them into subtasks:
  br create "Subtask title" -t task -p <priority> --description "..."
  br dep add <subtask-id> <parent-id> --type parent-child

Each subtask should be a single coherent unit of work that one agent can complete independently.
```

### 2. Update beadRefinementPrompt() in src/prompts.ts
Add polish question:
  '7. Are any beads too large? Could they be split into 2-3 subtasks for better parallelism and clearer scope?'

### 3. Update bead display in approve.ts
- When displaying beads in the approval UI, group subtasks under their parent:
  ```
  **parent-id: Parent title**
     ↳ child-1: Subtask one
     ↳ child-2: Subtask two
  **standalone-id: Standalone bead**
  ```
- Read bead.parent field (already in Bead type) to determine hierarchy
- Standalone beads (no parent, not a parent) display at top level

### 4. Handle subtask lifecycle in review.ts
- When a subtask is reviewed and closed, check if all sibling subtasks under the same parent are also closed
- If all siblings closed, auto-close the parent bead with summary: 'All subtasks complete'
- If not all closed, mention remaining subtasks in the review response

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill creates 'tasks, subtasks, and dependency structure.' Flat beads work for small plans but complex features benefit from decomposition. Subtasks enable better parallelism (each subtask goes to a separate agent) and clearer scope.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] beadCreationPrompt includes subtask creation instructions with br dep add --type parent-child
- [ ] beadRefinementPrompt asks about beads that should be split
- [ ] approve.ts groups subtasks under parents in display
- [ ] Parent auto-closes when all subtasks close
- [ ] Standalone beads unaffected
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

title: Define OrchestratorContext interface and shared runtime state
Add quality checklist gate before implementation
description:
Add an automated quality checklist that validates beads against the beads-workflow skill's 8-item checklist before allowing implementation to start.

## What to implement

### 1. Add qualityCheckBeads() to src/beads.ts
Run these checks on each open bead:
1. Self-contained: description length > 100 chars (heuristic for substance)
2. Clear scope: has a '### Files:' section with at least one file
3. Dependencies explicit: bead either has deps or is depended upon (or is the only bead)
4. Testable: description contains acceptance criteria markers ('- [ ]' or 'Acceptance criteria')
5. Includes tests: at least some beads have test-related content ('test', 'spec', 'verify')
6. Preserves features: (manual — flag for user review)
7. Not oversimplified: description word count > 50 (heuristic)
8. No cycles: br dep cycles returns empty

Return { passed: boolean, failures: { beadId, check, reason }[] }

### 2. Run quality check in approve.ts before 'Start implementing'
- After user clicks 'Start implementing', run qualityCheckBeads()
- If failures exist, display them and ask: 'Quality issues found. Proceed anyway?' with option to go back to polish
- If all pass, proceed normally

### 3. Display quality score in bead list
- Show pass/fail count: '✅ 6/8 quality checks passed'
- Highlight failing beads with specific issues

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill has an explicit quality checklist that gates implementation. Without it, shallow or incomplete beads get through to agents, causing implementation failures and rework.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] qualityCheckBeads() validates all 8 items (or close heuristic approximations)
- [ ] Quality gate runs before implementation starts
- [ ] Failures displayed with specific bead IDs and reasons
- [ ] User can override and proceed anyway

## Why it matters
CASS is only useful if context is injected at the right moments. Currently memory is injected at profile time and learnings saved at completion — same touchpoints, richer data.

title: Extract tools into src/tools/ directory
description:
Extract the 6 tool handlers from index.ts into separate files under src/tools/.

## Tools to extract:
- src/tools/profile.ts — orch_profile (index.ts lines ~497-662)
- src/tools/discover.ts — orch_discover (lines ~663-805)
- src/tools/select.ts — orch_select (lines ~806-992)  
- src/tools/approve.ts — orch_approve_beads (lines ~993-1195)
- src/tools/review.ts — orch_review (lines ~1496-1893)
- src/tools/memory-tool.ts — orch_memory (lines ~1404-1495)

## Pattern
Each file exports a registerXTool function:
```typescript
import type { OrchestratorContext } from '../types.js';
export function registerProfileTool(oc: OrchestratorContext) {
  oc.pi.registerTool({ name: 'orch_profile', ... });
}
```

index.ts calls each register function:
```typescript
registerProfileTool(oc);
registerDiscoverTool(oc);
// etc
```

## Key dependencies per tool:
- profile: scanRepo, detectCoordinationBackend, formatRepoProfile, discoveryInstructions, readMemory
- discover: state.candidateIdeas, CandidateIdea type
- select: runGoalRefinement, extractConstraints, beadCreationPrompt
- approve: readBeads, readyBeads, extractArtifacts, validateBeads, beadRefinementPrompt, implementerInstructions, agentMailTaskPreamble
- review: getBeadById, readyBeads, updateBeadStatus, syncBeads, implementerInstructions, runGuidedGates (from gates.ts), mergeWorktreeChanges, agentMailTaskPreamble
- memory: listMemoryEntries, searchMemory, getMemoryStats

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] src/tools/ directory with 6 files
- [ ] index.ts reduced to <200 lines (imports, context creation, registration calls, event hooks)
- [ ] All tools work exactly as before
- [ ] npm run build passes
- [ ] npm test passes

title: Prompt hardening: ultrathink, self-containment, sync after polish
description:
Three small prompt/behavior changes merged into one bead since they all touch src/prompts.ts and src/tools/approve.ts with no conflicts.

## What to implement

### 1. Add ultrathink directive (src/prompts.ts)

- Append "\n\nUse ultrathink." to the return value of beadRefinementPrompt()
- This triggers extended thinking in compatible models for deeper analysis during planning

### 2. Strengthen self-containment (src/prompts.ts)
- In beadCreationPrompt(), add to Requirements section:
  'The beads should be so detailed that a fresh agent never needs to consult back to the original goal. Include relevant background, reasoning/justification, considerations — anything a future agent needs about goals, intentions, and thought process.'
- In beadRefinementPrompt(), add question #7:
  '7. Could a fresh agent implement this bead without ANY external context? If not, what background/reasoning is missing?'

### 3. Sync beads after each polish round (src/tools/approve.ts)
- In the refinement branch of orch_approve_beads (where choice starts with '🔍'), call syncBeads(oc.pi, ctx.cwd) BEFORE returning the refinement prompt
- This ensures polish changes survive process death between rounds
- Import syncBeads if not already imported (it already is in approve.ts)

## Why it matters
These are all trivial, zero-risk changes from the beads-workflow skill gap analysis. Grouping them avoids 3 separate beads for 3 one-liner changes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] beadCreationPrompt() ends with 'Use ultrathink.'
- [ ] beadRefinementPrompt() ends with 'Use ultrathink.'
- [ ] beadCreationPrompt() includes self-containment paragraph in Requirements
- [ ] beadRefinementPrompt() includes question #7 about external context
- [ ] syncBeads called after each refinement pass in approve.ts
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/prompts.ts, src/tools/approve.ts

title: Cross-model bead review
description:
Add a cross-model review option during bead polishing that sends beads to an alternative model for a second opinion.

## What to implement

### 1. Add 'Cross-model review' option in approve.ts polish loop
- New UI choice: '🔀 Cross-model review' alongside 'Polish again' and 'Start implementing'
- Only show this option when polishRound >= 1 (at least one polish pass done first)

### 2. Create crossModelBeadReview() in src/bead-review.ts (new file)
Why a new file: deep-plan.ts runs agents via `pi --print --no-extensions --tools read,bash,grep,find,ls`. Bead review needs br CLI access to read beads and suggest changes. Different tool set, different file.

Implementation:
- Read all open beads via readBeads()
- Format as a review prompt: list each bead's title, description, deps, and files
- Ask: 'Review these beads for: gaps in coverage, oversimplifications, missing dependencies, unclear scope, beads that should be split or merged. Output specific suggestions as a numbered list.'
- Run via `pi --print --tools read,bash,br` with an alternative model (use the model selection from deep-plan config or default to a different provider)
- Parse response and return as structured { suggestions: string[], rawOutput: string }

### 3. Present results and optionally apply
- Display suggestions to user in the approval UI
- User chooses: 'Apply suggestions (send back to LLM)' or 'Ignore and continue'
- If applying: inject suggestions into the next polish round's beadRefinementPrompt as extra context
- Count as a polish round (increment polishRound)

### 4. Model selection
- Check if deep-plan.ts agents config exists (it has model names)
- If not, use a hardcoded fallback model that's different from the current session's model
- The point is to get a DIFFERENT model's perspective, not the same one

## Why it matters
The beads-workflow skill requires 'at least one alternative model reviewed' before beads are ready. Different models catch different blind spots — Claude might miss what Gemini catches and vice versa.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] 'Cross-model review' option appears after round 1
- [ ] crossModelBeadReview() sends beads to alternative model
- [ ] Suggestions displayed to user
- [ ] User can apply (inject into next polish) or ignore
- [ ] Review tracked as a polish round
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] npm test passes

Include relevant background, reasoning/justification, considerations, and anything a future agent needs to know about goals, intentions, thought process, and how each bead serves the overarching project.'
